metadata.working_title: Benchmarking DNA Foundation Models for Genomic and Genetic Tasks

abstract_sketch.problem: DNA foundation language models promise a unified representation for genomic and genetic analysis, but current evaluations are confounded by fine-tuning choices and narrow task scope.
abstract_sketch.gap: No comprehensive zero-shot benchmark exists that controls for pooling strategy, spans diverse species and biological feature types, and jointly evaluates sequence classification, gene expression prediction, variant effect quantification, and chromatin structure.
abstract_sketch.approach: We evaluated five DNA foundation models (DNABERT-2, NT-v2, HyenaDNA, Caduceus-Ph, GROVER) in a zero-shot embedding paradigm on 57 classification datasets, a GTEx v8 whole-blood gene expression panel, paired reference/alternative variant sequences for pathogenic SNPs and four classes of QTLs, a controlled pre-training data experiment, and a TAD attention analysis.
abstract_sketch.key_results: Mean token pooling consistently outperformed summary-token and maximum pooling (AUC gains of 1.4-8.7%); general DNA foundation models outperformed specialized Enformer for pathogenic variant identification (NT-v2 AUC 0.73 vs 0.69), while specialized models dominated QTL tasks (AlphaGenome sQTL AUC 0.80, ipaQTL AUC 0.86); re-pretraining HyenaDNA on 135-species data improved 14/49 datasets; NT-v2 attention showed no evidence of TAD recognition.
abstract_sketch.impact: The benchmark provides quantitative, task-specific guidance for choosing DNA foundation models and establishes mean token pooling as a best-practice default, while exposing current limits of self-supervised DNA models on chromatin structure and QTL prediction.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Motivate DNA foundation models as analogues of NLP foundation models for genomics.

Summarise five representative models (DNABERT-2, NT-v2, HyenaDNA, Caduceus-Ph, GROVER) spanning BERT-style encoders, Hyena operators and MambaDNA state-space backbones with different tokenisations and pre-training corpora.
Articulate the evaluation bias problem: fine-tuning confounds, narrow task scope, inconsistent pooling.

State our contributions: zero-shot benchmark across 57 classification datasets, variant effect and QTL tasks, gene expression prediction, controlled multi-species pre-training study, and attention-based chromatin analysis.

sections.title: Related Work
content_bullets: NLP foundation models and their transfer to biology.
DNA language models: DNABERT family, Nucleotide Transformer, HyenaDNA, Caduceus/MambaDNA, GROVER.
Specialised genomic predictors: Enformer, Sei, AlphaGenome.
Prior DNA model benchmarks and their limitations (fine-tuning bias, narrow scope, inconsistent pooling).

subsections.title: Mean token pooling consistently improves sequence classification
content_bullets: 52 binary classification datasets; mean > summary-token in 35-42/52 datasets per model (DeLong's test, p<0.01).
Average AUC gains 1.4%-8.7%; HyenaDNA largest (8.7%, IQR 4.6%-12.9%); GROVER smallest (1.4%, IQR 0.7%-1.9%).
Specific examples: GM12878 promoter DNABERT-2 0.964 to 0.986 (2.3%); B. amyloliquefaciens HyenaDNA 0.689 to 0.864 (25.4%).
Mean pooling moves min-max average AUC envelope from 0.708-0.799 to 0.795-0.822.

subsections.title: Human genome region classification
content_bullets: All five models AUC > 0.8 on the majority of tasks.
Caduceus-Ph best overall on human tasks; DNABERT-2 strongest on splice donor (0.906) and acceptor (0.897).
Four models beat baseline CNN on GM12878 and HUVEC promoter identification.

subsections.title: Multi-species genome region classification
content_bullets: HyenaDNA best on Arabidopsis TATA (0.961) and NonTATA (0.955) promoters despite human-only pre-training.
Caduceus-Ph 0.992 and GROVER 0.984 on human vs. worm classification.
DNA foundation models generally underperform baseline CNN on multispecies datasets.

subsections.title: Epigenetic modification prediction
content_bullets: AUC typically 10-15% below functional-region tasks.
Human 5mC: NT-v2 0.738, Caduceus-Ph 0.783, GROVER 0.744.
Eukaryotic 4mC: NT-v2 leads (A. thaliana 0.633, C. elegans 0.649, D. melanogaster 0.652); E. coli 4mC Caduceus-Ph 0.628.
Multiple foundation models outperform baseline CNN on yeast H3, H3K4me1, H3K79me3 marks.

subsections.title: Gene expression prediction from sequence
content_bullets: GTEx v8 whole blood, 610 subjects, 21,004 genes (short) and 768 genes (long).
Random forest significantly outperforms XGBoost (p < 0.001) across all models.
Average Pearson r 0.114-0.123 for 6 kb inputs.
HyenaDNA-450K significantly outperforms Enformer (p<0.01); longer inputs help HyenaDNA (p<0.001) but not Caduceus-Ph.
CUTALP consistently > 0.89 across all five short-sequence models; DDX11, HLA-DRB5 top across long-sequence models.

subsections.title: Pathogenic variant identification
content_bullets: 22,222 pathogenic / 17,374 common SNPs (long); 22,239 / 17,398 (short); 3 chromosome-based nested CV folds.
NT-v2 AUC 0.73, Cohen's d 0.88; Caduceus-Ph 0.70; outperforming Enformer (0.69, d=0.73) and Sei.
Short-sequence Caduceus-Ph (0.70) beats long-sequence Caduceus-Ph (0.62).

subsections.title: QTL prediction
content_bullets: 1,896 eQTLs, 540 sQTLs, 116 ipaQTLs, 142 paQTLs from GTEx v8 fine-mapped data.
AlphaGenome dominates: sQTL AUC 0.80, ipaQTL AUC 0.86.
Enformer eQTL AUC 0.77; Caduceus-Ph 0.65; Sei hidden-state sQTL 0.65 vs output 0.63.
Several DNA foundation models at or below chance on smaller QTL sets; performance highly sensitive to chromosome holdout.

subsections.title: Controlled pre-training data experiment
content_bullets: Re-pretrained HyenaDNA on DNABERT-2 multi-species corpus (135 species, 6 taxonomic categories).
Multi-species significantly better on 14/49 datasets (p<0.01); original better on 3/49.
Human 5mC 0.707 to 0.749; human vs. worm 0.968 to 0.984.
Original retains edge on human enhancer/open chromatin and yeast epigenetic marks.

subsections.title: TAD structure is not captured by self-attention alone
content_bullets: 1500 TAD-centered vs 1500 background sequences at 6000 bp; attention averaged across layers/heads of NT-v2.
Attention difference matrix near uniformly zero except diagonal; no TAD-specific pattern.
Other models excluded for attention-access reasons.

subsections.title: Runtime scaling
content_bullets: Single A100, 1000 replications, batch size 1, 64-524,288 nt.
HyenaDNA-160K stable below 2K nt; HyenaDNA-1M scales well up to ~500K nt.
NT-v2 highest absolute runtime (~2.5 s/100 reps); GROVER fastest within 2K nt.
DNABERT-2 competitive near 2K nt ceiling; Caduceus-Ph-131K moderate.

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: Pooling is a first-class design variable: mean pooling should be the default.
General DNA foundation models can beat specialized predictors on SNP-level pathogenicity yet lose to them on QTL tasks — a capability/data-resolution trade-off.
Multi-species pre-training helps epigenetic and cross-species tasks but does not uniformly dominate.
Current models do not learn TAD-scale structure without fine-tuning; architecture with accessible long-range attention is needed.
Runtime/accuracy trade-offs favor HyenaDNA for long sequences and NT-v2 for quality-critical short tasks.
Limitations: single tissue (whole blood), limited QTL sample sizes, attention analysis restricted to NT-v2.

subsections.title: Foundation model checkpoints
content_bullets: Five models, parameters, embedding dim, max length, tokenisation (per Models table).

subsections.title: Embedding pooling and classifiers
content_bullets: Final-layer tokens; summary-token, mean, max; random forest primary, naive Bayes, elastic-net; baseline CNN.

subsections.title: Classification tasks
content_bullets: 52 binary + 5 multi-class; AUROC; DeLong's test p<0.01; bolding rule.

subsections.title: Gene expression setup
content_bullets: GTEx v8 whole blood; 610 subjects; TSS +/- 3 kb and +/- 98 kb; residualized expression; RF vs XGBoost.

subsections.title: Variant effect quantification
content_bullets: Paired ref/alt sequences; pathogenic vs common SNP counts; QTL counts; nested CV by chromosome.

subsections.title: HyenaDNA multi-species re-pretraining
content_bullets: DNABERT-2 multi-species corpus; matched hyperparameters to HyenaDNA-1K.

subsections.title: TAD attention analysis
content_bullets: Top 5% boundary strength; 1500 TAD vs 1500 background 6 kb sequences; attention averaged across layers/heads.

subsections.title: Runtime benchmarking
content_bullets: A100; 1000 replications; batch 1; 64-524,288 nt.
